#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

content.text:
Cannot reproduce. Ran `FILTER='^handoff_roundtrip$' bash run_e2e.sh` 5x in a row -- all pass with rc=3. Manual repro (drain QEMU 1 -> spawn vsim with `--qemu-binary`) also reaches `[info] hybrid handback: continuing target` and exits 3 cleanly.

The original 124 means `timeout 60` killed vsim. Log stopped after `connecting to gdbstub on tcp::37557` -- that's stderr block-buffered to file, so the actual hang point is later (after restore, while waiting on QEMU 2's semihosting exit). Likely transient: port reuse race, or QEMU 2 wedged.

Re: "turn off the strace" -- did you mean the gdbstub (`-gdb tcp::PORT -S`)? That's the handback contract; vsim spawns QEMU 2 frozen specifically so it can restore GPRs/PC/CSRs over the gdbstub before `c`. Removing `-S` would race the restore against execution. If you want to run QEMU 2 manually, drop `--qemu-binary` from vsim and have it just write `--handoff-out`, then launch QEMU yourself however you want. Want me to add a `--no-handback` (or similar) flag for that?

Or did you mean something else by "strace"?

content.text:
No, you don't need it.

The QEMU at `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64` is a plain ELF, no strace wrapper baked in. `run_e2e.sh` invokes it directly. The strace wrapping is only Claude's sandbox concern -- when you run the script in your own shell, just `bash run_e2e.sh` works.

If you have `VSIM_QEMU` exported to a sandbox-wrapper path in your env, `unset VSIM_QEMU` (or override to `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`) before running.

message.content: so, maybe that's the root cause why I got the error message

content.text:
Probably not. The spawn line in your failing log shows the plain binary path:

```
spawning /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 -gdb tcp::37557 -S
```

No strace wrapper there. vsim `execv`s that path directly, so whatever Claude's sandbox wraps externally wouldn't apply to the QEMU 2 child.

That said, I can't reproduce it (5/5 pass). If it recurs, run with `VSIM_LOG_LEVEL=debug` (or whatever your spdlog gate is) and unbuffered stderr -- `2>&1 | cat` instead of `>file 2>&1` -- to see exactly which step hangs after `connecting to gdbstub`.

Found it. Two stale `bash run_e2e.sh` are stuck in `do_signal_stop` (you Ctrl-Z'd a hung run earlier), and their vsim/QEMU children are also frozen in state T. They're not zombies - they're suspended. That explains why a fresh `./run_e2e.sh` is starving for resources/ports.

Clean them first:

```
kill -CONT 2521478 2542039 ; kill -TERM 2521478 2542039 ; pkill -KILL -u $USER -f sim_ax45mpv_premium ; pkill -KILL -u $USER -f 'qemu-system-riscv64.*-gdb tcp::'
```

After that, the rc=124 itself: the visible `[info] hybrid handback: connecting to gdbstub` is the *last flushed* line, not the hang point - spdlog is block-buffered to a file, so anything after `connect_loopback()` returned is in lost buffer when `timeout` SIGTERMs vsim.

To pin the actual stall point, re-run handoff_roundtrip alone with stderr line-buffered:

```
FILTER='^handoff_roundtrip$' stdbuf -oL -eL bash run_e2e.sh
```

# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so

content.text:
I think I have the root cause.

The key difference: my Bash tool runs the script with **stdin = /dev/null**, your interactive shell runs it with **stdin = /dev/pts/25**.

Your earlier stuck processes were in state `T` with `wchan = do_signal_stop` — that's `SIGTTIN`. QEMU 2 spawned with `-nographic` multiplexes monitor+serial on stdio. `timeout(1)` puts vsim+QEMU 2 in a *new* process group (not the terminal's foreground group). When QEMU 2 reads stdin from `/dev/pts/25` while not foreground → SIGTTIN → stopped → vsim hangs in waitpid → `timeout 60` fires → rc=124.

Quick smoking-gun test, run this in your terminal:

```
FILTER='^handoff_roundtrip$' bash run_e2e.sh </dev/null
```

If it passes, we've found it. Real fix is in `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` `spawn_qemu()` - redirect child stdin to `/dev/null` before `execv`, so QEMU 2 never blocks on the tty regardless of who launched the suite.

content.text:
Confirmed. Root cause:

`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` `spawn_qemu()` forks QEMU 2 inheriting stdin from vsim. `timeout(1)` puts the vsim+QEMU 2 group in a new pgid (not the terminal's foreground group). QEMU's `-nographic` multiplexes monitor+serial on stdio → reads stdin → kernel sends `SIGTTIN` to a non-foreground pgid reader → stopped (state T, `wchan=do_signal_stop`) → vsim's `waitpid` blocks → `timeout 60` fires → rc=124. The `</dev/null` test bypasses it because EOF returns 0 instead of triggering tty access.

Fix: in the child branch of `spawn_qemu()` (before `execv`), `dup2(open("/dev/null", O_RDONLY), STDIN_FILENO)`. Single, local, no behavior change for non-tty callers.

TDD path:
1. RED: a test that spawns vsim under a fresh pgid with a real pty as stdin (e.g., via `forkpty`), drives one handoff_roundtrip, asserts exit==3 within ~10s. Will hang/time-out before fix.
2. GREEN: the `dup2` change.
3. Commit as `behavioral:` (changes runtime).

Want me to implement the test + fix on the `hybrid` branch of `hybrid_vsim`?

  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES

      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Reproduces the SIGTTIN hang of QEMU 2 when vsim is launched from a
# non-foreground process group under a real pty.
#
# Without the spawn_qemu stdin redirect:
#   - timeout(1) places vsim+QEMU 2 in a new pgid that is NOT the pty's
#     foreground group.
#   - QEMU 2's -nographic stdio reads the pty, the kernel delivers SIGTTIN,
#     QEMU 2 stops, vsim's waitpid blocks, the outer timeout fires (rc=124).
#
# With the fix (child stdin -> /dev/null in spawn_qemu): no tty access,
# no SIGTTIN, vsim completes the round-trip normally (rc=3).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

if ! command -v script >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "skip: script(1) not available"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
WRAPPER=$(mktemp --suffix=.sh)
chmod +x "$WRAPPER"
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG" "$WRAPPER"' EXIT

"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

cat >"$WRAPPER" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
exec timeout 20 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \\
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \\
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \\
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
EOF

# script(1) allocates a fresh pty and runs WRAPPER inside it. The wrapper's
# non-interactive bash skips tcsetpgrp, so timeout's pgid is non-foreground:
# the precise condition that exposes the SIGTTIN bug.
set +e
script -qc "$WRAPPER" /dev/null >/dev/null
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 3 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: expected exit 3, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_pty_handback] PASS: vsim exit=$RC under pty"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Reproduces the SIGTTIN hang of QEMU 2 when vsim is launched from a
# non-foreground process group under a real pty.
#
# Without the spawn_qemu stdin redirect:
#   - timeout(1) places vsim+QEMU 2 in a new pgid that is NOT the pty's
#     foreground group.
#   - QEMU 2's -nographic stdio reads the pty, the kernel delivers SIGTTIN,
#     QEMU 2 stops, vsim's waitpid blocks, the outer timeout fires (rc=124).
#
# With the fix (child stdin -> /dev/null in spawn_qemu): no tty access,
# no SIGTTIN, vsim completes the round-trip normally (rc=3).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

if ! command -v script >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "skip: script(1) not available"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
WRAPPER=$(mktemp --suffix=.sh)
chmod +x "$WRAPPER"
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG" "$WRAPPER"' EXIT

"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"

fi

cat >"$WRAPPER" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
exec timeout 20 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \\
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \\
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \\
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
EOF

RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 3 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: expected exit 3, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
podman run --rm \
  -v "$PWD:/work" \
  -v "$ROOT/include:/include:ro" \
  -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh
